questions.question: Explain the difference between attitudinal and behavioral research methods with examples.
expectedPoints: Attitudinal research focuses on what people say/think (e.g., interviews, surveys)
Behavioral research focuses on what people actually do (e.g., usability testing, analytics)
Why both are important for comprehensive understanding

questions.question: Describe how you would plan and conduct a usability study for a new mobile banking application. Include recruitment strategies, methodology, tasks, and metrics.
expectedPoints: Task design for mobile banking context
Key metrics to collect (success rate, time on task, error rate)
How findings would inform design iterations
